headline: Reuters: Carwow Germany survey (n=1,164, Mar 12) — 48% say spiking fuel prices would influence EV/hybrid purchase decision

Carwow also reports up to 66% of shoppers looking at EVs Mar 2-12 vs 55% end of Feb. MeinAuto EV-related traffic +40% since war began. First quantified survey-stated EV shift in EU since crisis began.

headline: Reuters: UK used-EV dealer (EV Experts, near London) logs busiest Saturday ever one week post-war; staff buying EVs at auctions 'like mad'
data_point: Martin Miller, owner of EV Experts used-EV dealership southwest of London. Busiest Saturday on record one week after war began. 'We're turning cars very, very quickly.' Customers concerned petrol prices will climb higher. Staff buying more EVs at auctions 'like mad — because we're confident this will continue.' Anecdotal but high-quality dealer-level signal.

headline: Reuters: Recharged used EV dealership (Richmond VA) — customer trades ICE SUV for EV plus a second smaller EV: 'trying to get in before everybody freaks out'
data_point: Customer Zach Xavier visited Recharged dealership in Richmond, Virginia with wife on Friday Mar 13 to trade in combustion-engine SUV for electric one and purchased a second smaller EV. Quote: 'I'm trying to get in before everybody freaks out.' Anecdotal but illustrative of pre-emptive switching behavior in US heartland markets.

headline: Pew Research (Mar 16-22, n=3,524): 32% of Americans would seriously consider EV — flat YoY despite gas crisis; hybrids 44%
data_point: 32% would seriously consider an EV as next vehicle (vs 33% March 2025 = -1pp); 44% would consider a hybrid (also flat YoY, about the same as 2024). Partisan: Democrats 47% (down from 58% in 2022, -11pp over 3 years), Republicans 17%. 60% of Democrats vs 21% of Republicans say govt should encourage EV use. Survey conducted Mar 16-22, 2026 — fully overlapping the post-war fuel price spike. STRONG counter-narrative: US showed zero crisis lift in stated consideration for EITHER EVs or hybrids despite gasoline +27-39% in March.
